title: crotalaria irwinii
intro: Crotalaria irwinii, also known as Irwin's rattlebox, is a flowering plant native to Australia. It is a member of the pea family (Fabaceae) and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antimalarial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, Crotalaria irwinii can be made into a tea, extract, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
